data.description:
## Why
The strongest decomposition-shape consensus in the SOTA report (vertical slices over layer-cake splits; CodePlan integration-truth; Fowler's over-decomposition counter-example) has no criterion today: a DB/backend/frontend horizontal split passes G5 and G3. Shape defects are real but judgment-heavy, so this ships ADVISORY-ONLY regardless of detection power (epic gate table).

## What
A container-review decomposition-shape criterion (pass-1, advisory posture permanently): flags (a) layer-cake child splits where no child is an independently-shippable vertical slice, and (b) consumed-artifact-without-ordering-edge (child consumes a sibling's output with no dependency edge - the P5/G4 gap). Implement ONLY if the E2 gate row passes: >=7/10 layer-cake+ordering mutants detected AND <=1/10 controls flagged; E6's order-shuffle stability check must also hold for the new container rubric. Incorporated directly into the packaged registry on pass.

## Scope
- src/rebar/llm/plan_review/criteria_routing.json (new advisory criterion) + a new reviewers/ rubric prompt (+ index/guide regeneration).
- docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/ gate-row writeup consuming E2/E6 artifacts.
- Sequenced AFTER the floor-split story (large-sleepful-needlefish) to avoid criteria_routing.json conflicts.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Gate-row evidence committed before landing: >=7/10 E2 layer-cake/ordering mutants detected, <=1/10 controls flagged, E6 order-shuffle stability recorded for the rubric.
- [ ] The criterion lands advisory in the packaged registry with default_posture advisory and no blocking path (no config flag), and the guide/index regeneration is included.
- [ ] docs/plan-review-gate.md documents the criterion and its permanent advisory posture rationale.
- [ ] If the gate row fails, the story closes deferred with the failing numbers recorded.
data.title: R3: decomposition-shape container criterion (advisory-only, gated on E2+E6)
